finding_results.notes: Removed narrator assertion ('The professional polish in his voice was cracking now. More Birmingham came through when he spoke fast.') and replaced with audible stress markers in dialogue. Line 33 adds colloquial connector ('Right, so') and British phrasing ('has got'). Line 37 introduces two self-corrections: an interrupted restart ('enough to cause— intermittent disruption, right') and a mid-clause correction ('it's not— it hasn't cascaded'), plus colloquial 'firmware's got.' Line 41 adds British profanity ('bloody'), informal shortening ('the bay'), colloquial verb ('hooking them up'), and private-register connector ('So'). Petra's lines remain clinically steady, making the register contrast audible without narrator instruction.

summary: Single finding addressed. Yusuf's stress register in the Petra exchange now surfaces through dialogue mechanics — interrupted restarts, self-corrections, colloquial connectors, and a British profanity marker — rather than through narrator assertion. The register contrast between Yusuf's cracking composure and Petra's clinical steadiness is carried entirely by the speech itself. No changes to Lena/Kai or Margot sections; all other dialogue checked against style bible contraction levels and found character-true.
